headlines.description: Alexander Isak's early headed goal lifted Newcastle to a 1-0 victory over Arsenal on Saturday at St. James' Park, as Eddie Howe's men delivered a blow to Arsenal's title chase with their first win in six Premier League outings.
headlines.shortLinkText: Isak header puts huge dent in Arsenal's title hopes

headlines.description: Antoine Semenyo and Evanilson got on the scoresheet as Bournemouth held on for a shock 2-1 victory over champions Manchester City on Saturday.
headlines.shortLinkText: City lose unbeaten run in shock Bournemouth loss

headlines.description: Leicester City denied 10-man Ipswich Town their first win of the Premier League season on Saturday when a stunning second-half volley from left back Leif Davis was cancelled out by a late Jordan Ayew goal for the visitors.
headlines.shortLinkText: Late Leicester equaliser denies Ipswich first win

headlines.description: A sensational trademark strike from Mohamed Salah earned Liverpool a 2-1 comeback victory over Brighton & Hove Albion on Saturday, a win that moved Arne Slot's team two points clear at the top of the Premier League.
headlines.shortLinkText: Salah stunner in comeback win sends L'pool top

events.name: West Ham United at Nottingham Forest

headlines.description: Nottingham Forest made the most of the sending-off of West Ham United's Edson Alvarez with a rip-roaring 3-0 win on Saturday that lifted them into third spot the Premier League, with Chris Wood, Callum Hudson-Odoi and Ola Aina getting the goals.
headlines.shortLinkText: Forest go 3rd as Wood scores in West Ham win

headlines.description: Promoted Southampton finally got to celebrate a first Premier League win of the season with a 1-0 victory over Everton thanks to a late Adam Armstrong goal.
headlines.shortLinkText: Southampton earn first win of season vs. Everton

headlines.description: Wolverhampton Wanderers remained winless and still bottom of the Premier League after an entertaining 2-2 draw at home to Crystal Palace on Saturday.
headlines.shortLinkText: Wolves stay bottom after home draw with Palace
